mnemonics: Naive Bayes Training: Count, Calculate, Classify (The 3 C's of Naive Bayes)
Priori Probability = Prior Knowledge. Likelihood = Learned from Data.
Smoothing Saves Sparsity (Add-1 smoothing prevents zero probabilities from missing words).
keyPoints: The model is trained by calculating prior probabilities P(c) for each class and likelihoods P(feature|c) for each feature given a class.
The 'naive' assumption is that all features (words) are conditionally independent given the class label.
Laplace (Add-1) smoothing is essential to handle words in the test set that were not seen in the training set for a particular class, preventing zero probabilities.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to apply Laplace smoothing, which causes the entire probability product to become zero if a single unknown word is encountered.
Misinterpreting the 'naive' assumption to mean the features are independent of each other in general, rather than only being conditionally independent given the class.
Confusing the prior probability P(c) (how frequent a class is) with the likelihood P(word|c) (how frequent a word is within that class).
